You are working on beads issue kapow-pr9.5: Contract traceability model.

## WORKTREE SETUP вЂ” RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/traceability-model"

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## Issue Details
**kapow-pr9.5: Contract traceability model**

Define the traceability model between contract entries, source files, acceptance tests, technical tests, and beads issues so automation can determine when protected changes require contract updates.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Traceability rules are defined and documented. Contract entries can reference tests, issues, and protected files in a machine-readable way. The model is specific enough for enforcement scripts to use without manual interpretation.

## Instructions
1. Read CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for conventions
2. Read `contracts/README.md` and `contracts/schema/` вЂ” the contract registry schemas are now merged
3. Read `docs/specs/contract-gates.md` for gate behavior
4. Create `docs/specs/contract-traceability.md` covering:
   - How contract entries link to source files (the `source_files` field)
   - How contract entries link to tests (the `tests` field)
   - How contract entries link to beads issues (the `issues` field)
   - Traceability rules: when a source file changes, which contracts need review
   - Enforcement model: how automation determines "this change requires contract update"
   - Protected file в†’ contract mapping patterns
   - Examples of traceability chains (source change в†’ contract в†’ test в†’ evidence)
5. Keep it practical вЂ” this document drives the contract traceability checker (kapow-n92.6)
6. Follow existing docs/specs/ style

## Quality Gate
Docs-only change. Run: `make test-quick`

## When Done
Commit and push:
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "docs: add contract traceability model documentation

Defines how contract entries link to source files, tests, and issues,
and how automation determines when protected changes require contract
updates.

Closes kapow-pr9.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git push -u origin HEAD
```
Report completion to team lead.
</teammate-message>

1в†’# Contract Registry
     2в†’
     3в†’Machine-readable registries that formalize what Kapow promises to users
     4в†’(product contracts) and what the system guarantees internally (technical
     5в†’contracts).
     6в†’
     7в†’## Directory structure
     8в†’
     9в†’```
    10в†’contracts/
    11в†’  schema/
    12в†’    product-contract.schema.json    # JSON Schema for product contracts
    13в†’    technical-contract.schema.json  # JSON Schema for technical contracts
    14в†’  examples/
    15в†’    product-contract.example.json   # Example: Institution Search feature
    16в†’    technical-contract.example.json # Example: Search Validation Parity
    17в†’  README.md                         # This file
    18в†’```
    19в†’
    20в†’## Contract types
    21в†’
    22в†’### Product contracts
    23в†’
    24в†’Define user-visible promises: features, pages, integrations, and data-quality
    25в†’guarantees. Each contract answers: "What can users do, and how do we know it
    26в†’works?"
    27в†’
    28в†’**Categories**: `feature`, `page`, `integration`, `data-quality`
    29в†’
    30в†’### Technical contracts
    31в†’
    32в†’Define system-level guarantees: API behavior invariants, search engine rules,
    33в†’auth requirements, data integrity constraints, and performance bounds. Each
    34в†’contract answers: "What does the system guarantee, and how do we prove it?"
    35в†’
    36в†’**Categories**: `api`, `search`, `auth`, `data`, `performance`, `infrastructure`
    37в†’
    38в†’## Field reference
    39в†’
    40в†’### Required fields (both types)
    41в†’
    42в†’| Field | Type | Description |
    43в†’|---|---|---|
    44в†’| `id` | string | Unique kebab-case identifier (e.g., `institution-search`) |
    45в†’| `title` | string | Human-readable name |
    46в†’| `description` | string | What this contract promises or guarantees |
    47в†’| `status` | enum | Lifecycle status (see below) |
    48в†’| `owner` | string | Responsible team or person |
    49в†’| `category` | enum | Contract category (type-specific, see above) |
    50в†’
    51в†’### Optional fields (both types)
    52в†’
    53в†’| Field | Type | Description |
    54в†’|---|---|---|
    55в†’| `source_files` | string[] | File paths (repo-relative) implementing this contract |
    56в†’| `tests` | string[] | Test files or test name patterns verifying this contract |
    57в†’| `issues` | string[] | Linked beads issue IDs (e.g., `kapow-4vy`) |
    58в†’| `evidence` | object | Verification method вЂ” see schema for structure |
    59в†’| `created` | date | Creation date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
    60в†’| `updated` | date | Last update date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
    61в†’
    62в†’### Product-only fields
    63в†’
    64в†’| Field | Type | Description |
    65в†’|---|---|---|
    66в†’| `user_story` | string | "As a ... I can ..." format |
    67в†’| `acceptance_criteria` | string[] | Conditions for fulfillment |
    68в†’
    69в†’### Technical-only fields
    70в†’
    71в†’| Field | Type | Description |
    72в†’|---|---|---|
    73в†’| `guarantees` | object[] | Specific guarantees with optional `metric` and `threshold` |
    74в†’| `dependencies` | string[] | IDs of contracts this one depends on |
    75в†’
    76в†’## Status lifecycle
    77в†’
    78в†’| Status | Meaning |
    79в†’|---|---|
    80в†’| `planned` | Defined but not yet implemented |
    81в†’| `experimental` | Implemented but not yet stable; may change without notice |
    82в†’| `shipped` | Stable and verified; changes require a migration plan |
    83в†’| `deprecated` | Scheduled for removal; replacement should be identified |
    84в†’
    85в†’Transitions: `planned` в†’ `experimental` в†’ `shipped` в†’ `deprecated`
    86в†’
    87в†’A contract should not skip `experimental` unless it ships with full test
    88в†’coverage from day one.
    89в†’
    90в†’## Evidence types
    91в†’
    92в†’| Type | When to use |
    93в†’|---|---|
    94в†’| `test` | Automated tests verify the contract (preferred) |
    95в†’| `assertion` | Runtime assertions enforce the contract |
    96в†’| `monitoring` | Dashboards or alerts detect violations |
    97в†’| `manual` | Human verification (last resort) |
    98в†’
    99в†’## Validation
   100в†’
   101в†’Schemas use JSON Schema draft-07. Validate contract files against the schemas:
   102в†’
   103в†’```bash
   104в†’# With Node.js (ajv-cli)
   105в†’npx ajv validate -s contracts/schema/product-contract.schema.json -d 'contracts/product/*.json'
   106в†’
   107в†’# With Python (jsonschema)
   108в†’python3 -c "
   109в†’import json, jsonschema
   110в†’schema = json.load(open('contracts/schema/product-contract.schema.json'))
   111в†’data = json.load(open('contracts/examples/product-contract.example.json'))
   112в†’jsonschema.validate(data, schema)
   113в†’print('Valid')
   114в†’"
   115в†’```
   116в†’
   117в†’## Adding a contract
   118в†’
   119в†’1. Choose the appropriate schema (product or technical)
   120в†’2. Create a JSON file following the schema
   121в†’3. Set `status` to `planned` or `experimental`
   122в†’4. Link `source_files` and `tests` to actual repo paths
   123в†’5. Add `evidence` describing how the contract is verified
   124в†’6. Validate against the schema before committing
   125в†’
No files found

1в†’# Agent Instructions
     2в†’
     3в†’This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
     4в†’
     5в†’## Quick Reference
     6в†’
     7в†’```bash
     8в†’bd ready              # Find available work
     9в†’bd show <id>          # View issue details
    10в†’bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
    11в†’bd close <id>         # Complete work
    12в†’bd sync               # Sync with git
    13в†’```
    14в†’
    15в†’## Issue Title Guidelines
    16в†’
    17в†’Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    18в†’is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    19в†’
    20в†’| Bad | Good |
    21в†’|---|---|
    22в†’| `Add support for filtering by SAT score` | `SAT score filter for search` |
    23в†’| `Update the search page to show result counts` | `Search result count display` |
    24в†’| `Fix bug where geographic radius is ignored` | `Geo radius filter ignored` |
    25в†’| `Implement new card component for institutions` | `Institution card component` |
    26в†’
    27в†’- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search вЂ¦", "Auth вЂ¦", "GeoField вЂ¦"), not filler verbs
    28в†’- **Under 50 characters** вЂ” put detail in `--description`
    29в†’- Avoid prefixes like "Add support for", "Update the", "Implement new"
    30в†’
    31в†’## Creating Issues
    32в†’
    33в†’Use the correct issue type:
    34в†’- `epic` вЂ” a grouping container for related features/tasks. Not directly workable.
    35в†’- `feature` вЂ” delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    36в†’- `task` вЂ” scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work (e.g., project init, CI setup)
    37в†’- `bug` вЂ” something broken that needs fixing
    38в†’
    39в†’### Creating Epics
    40в†’
    41в†’Epics must use `--waits-for-gate` so they are blocked until their children complete, and do not appear in `bd ready`:
    42в†’
    43в†’```bash
    44в†’bd create "Epic: Feature Area" -t epic -p 2 -l label1,label2 \
    45в†’  -d "Description of the epic" \
    46в†’  --waits-for-gate all-children
    47в†’```
    48в†’
    49в†’This makes the epic resolve automatically when all children are closed. Never leave an epic as a bare open issue with no gate вЂ” it will pollute `bd ready`.
    50в†’
    51в†’### Creating Child Issues Under Epics
    52в†’
    53в†’Use `--parent` to place issues under an epic, and `--waits-for` to make the epic wait for the child:
    54в†’
    55в†’```bash
    56в†’bd create "Implement feature X" -t feature -p 1 -l search,go \
    57в†’  --parent kapow-abc \
    58в†’  --waits-for kapow-abc \
    59в†’  -d "Description" \
    60в†’  --acceptance "Acceptance criteria"
    61в†’```
    62в†’
    63в†’The `--parent` flag establishes the hierarchy. The `--waits-for kapow-abc` flag tells the epic to wait for this child before it can close.
    64в†’
    65в†’### Dependencies Between Issues
    66в†’
    67в†’Use `bd dep add` to express ordering constraints between issues:
    68в†’
    69в†’```bash
    70в†’bd dep add kapow-child kapow-dependency   # kapow-child is blocked by kapow-dependency
    71в†’```
    72в†’
    73в†’Only add dependencies where there is a real technical ordering constraint (e.g., "the migration must exist before the resolver can query the new column"). Do not add dependencies for soft preferences or nice-to-have ordering.
    74в†’
    75в†’## Implementation Plans
    76в†’
    77в†’Claude Code saves plans to `~/.claude/plans/` with random names (e.g.,
    78в†’`replicated-drifting-meerkat.md`). These are local to the machine and lost
    79в†’when switching devices. **After a plan is approved, copy it into the repo**
    80в†’so it persists across machines and sessions.
    81в†’
    82в†’### Saving a plan to the repo
    83в†’
    84в†’After exiting plan mode, copy the plan file from `~/.claude/plans/` into
    85в†’`docs/plans/`, renaming it to something meaningful:
    86в†’
    87в†’```bash
    88в†’cp ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    89в†’```
    90в†’
    91в†’Examples:
    92в†’- `docs/plans/kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`
    93в†’- `docs/plans/auth-refactor.md` (if no issue exists yet)
    94в†’
    95в†’Use lowercase kebab-case. Prefer `<issue-id>-<short-name>` when an issue exists.
    96в†’
    97в†’### Referencing from issues
    98в†’
    99в†’Add a note to the beads issue pointing to the plan:
   100в†’
   101в†’```bash
   102в†’bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
   103в†’```
   104в†’
   105в†’### Resuming work from a plan
   106в†’
   107в†’When starting a new session, check `docs/plans/` for in-progress plans.
   108в†’If a beads issue references a plan file, read it before starting work.
   109в†’
   110в†’### Lifecycle
   111в†’
   112в†’- Commit and push plan files alongside the code they describe
   113в†’- Update plan files as the approach evolves during implementation
   114в†’- Delete plan files after the work is fully merged and closed
   115в†’
   116в†’## Completing an Issue
   117в†’
   118в†’An issue is not complete until:
   119в†’1. All code changes pass the required quality gate (see below)
   120в†’2. Changes are committed
   121в†’3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   122в†’4. Changes are pushed to remote with `bd sync && git push`
   123в†’
   124в†’## Quality Gates (Mandatory)
   125в†’
   126в†’Every code change MUST pass the appropriate quality gate **before committing**.
   127в†’Do not skip gates, use `--no-verify`, or treat failures as acceptable.
   128в†’
   129в†’### Required gate by change type
   130в†’
   131в†’| Change type | Gate | Command |
   132в†’|---|---|---|
   133в†’| Go code only | Standard | `make test-standard` |
   134в†’| Web code only | Standard | `make test-standard` |
   135в†’| Go + Web | Standard | `make test-standard` |
   136в†’| GraphQL schema (`.graphql`) | Standard + regenerate | `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync && make test-standard` |
   137в†’| Database migration | Full | `make test-full` |
   138в†’| Fixture data | Fixture | `make test-fixture` |
   139в†’| Docs only | Quick | `make test-quick` |
   140в†’
   141в†’**Rules:**
   142в†’- The gate must exit with **zero errors and zero warnings**
   143в†’- Run the gate from the repo root, not a subdirectory
   144в†’- If your change spans multiple types, use the **strictest** gate
   145в†’- When in doubt, run `make test-standard` вЂ” it covers most cases
   146в†’
   147в†’### Responding to gate failures
   148в†’
   149в†’1. **Read the full error output** вЂ” identify the root cause, not just the first line
   150в†’2. **Fix the code, not the test** вЂ” unless the test itself is wrong
   151в†’3. **Re-run the complete gate command** вЂ” partial re-runs miss cascading failures
   152в†’4. **If the failure is in generated code**, regenerate first: `make api-generate` and/or `make web-schema-sync`
   153в†’5. **Never bypass**: no `--no-verify`, no `--force`, no skipping the gate
   154в†’
   155в†’## Contract Test Rules
   156в†’
   157в†’The search validation contract (`api/internal/search/contract.go`) defines a
   158в†’shared test matrix consumed by three entry-point test runners:
   159в†’
   160в†’- `api/internal/search/contract_test.go` вЂ” baseline (ground truth)
   161в†’- `api/internal/handler/contract_test.go` вЂ” CSV export adapter
   162в†’- `api/internal/mcp/contract_test.go` вЂ” MCP protocol adapter
   163в†’
   164в†’### When you MUST update `ContractCases()`
   165в†’
   166в†’- Adding, removing, or renaming a search field
   167в†’- Changing validation logic in `ValidateRequest`
   168в†’- Adding a new entry point that consumes `SearchRequest`
   169в†’- Changing auth requirements for search fields
   170в†’
   171в†’### Contract update workflow
   172в†’
   173в†’1. Edit `api/internal/search/contract.go` вЂ” add/modify cases
   174в†’2. Run `make api-test` вЂ” all three contract runners must pass
   175в†’3. If a new entry point is added, create a corresponding `contract_test.go` that
   176в†’   consumes `ContractCases()` with the same assertion pattern
   177в†’
   178в†’**All three contract test files must pass.** A change that passes in one runner
   179в†’but fails in another indicates contract drift вЂ” fix the adapter, not the contract.
   180в†’
   181в†’## Protected Surfaces
   182в†’
   183в†’These areas require extra verification beyond the standard gate. Changes to
   184в†’protected surfaces that skip the additional checks MUST be rejected.
   185в†’
   186в†’| Surface | Extra requirement | Why |
   187в†’|---|---|---|
   188в†’| Generated files (`graph/generated/`, `graph/model/`, `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts`) | Never edit manually; always regenerate with `make api-generate` / `make web-schema-sync` | Manual edits are silently overwritten on next generate |
   189в†’| Migration files (`api/migrations/`) | `make test-full` with live DB; update fixtures if schema changes | Runtime pgx type errors only surface with a live connection |
   190в†’| Search field registry (`api/internal/search/registry.go`) | Update contract cases; verify `TestBuildSQLAllFields` passes | Contract drift across entry points |
   191в†’| Auth middleware (`api/internal/middleware/auth.go`) | Integration tests with real JWT validation | Auth bugs are silent and security-critical |
   192в†’| GraphQL schema (`api/graph/schema/*.graphql`) | `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync` before any other checks | Stale generated code causes confusing type errors |
   193в†’| Fixture data (`common/src/main/resources/data/`) | `make test-fixture` | Fixture/schema mismatch breaks all integration tests |
   194в†’
   195в†’## Git Workflow
   196в†’
   197в†’When work is complete and verified (tests pass, build succeeds, lint is clean),
   198в†’merge directly into `main`. Only fall back to a pull request if there is an
   199в†’unresolvable problem merging from the command line (e.g. complex conflicts).
   200в†’
   201в†’### Commit messages and issue references
   202в†’
   203в†’When a commit includes changes to beads data (`.beads/` files), list all
   204в†’affected issue IDs in the commit message body. This makes it easy to trace
   205в†’which issues were created, updated, or closed in each push.
   206в†’
   207в†’Example:
   208в†’```
   209в†’Add geo search field and update filter UI
   210в†’
   211в†’Implements proximity-based search using PostGIS ST_DWithin.
   212в†’
   213в†’Issues: kapow-a1b, kapow-c2d, kapow-e3f
   214в†’```
   215в†’
   216в†’### Worktree workflow
   217в†’
   218в†’When working in a git worktree (e.g. started with `--worktree`), **always merge
   219в†’the branch back into `main` before ending the session.** Do not leave the
   220в†’worktree branch unmerged without asking.
   221в†’
   222в†’**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   223в†’- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   224в†’- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging: `git rebase main` (from the worktree)
   225в†’
   226в†’**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   227в†’- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   228в†’- `.gitattributes` marks generated files with `merge=ours` вЂ” conflicts auto-resolve to the current branch, but you must regenerate to get the correct content
   229в†’
   230в†’**Worktree cleanup вЂ” never for Claude-managed worktrees:**
   231в†’Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically вЂ” both for `--worktree`
   232в†’sessions and for subagents using `isolation: "worktree"`. These worktrees are
   233в†’cleaned up on session/agent exit.
   234в†’
   235в†’**Rules:**
   236в†’- **Never** run `rm -rf` on any worktree directory вЂ” use `git worktree remove` instead, which properly updates git bookkeeping
   237в†’- **Never** run `git worktree remove` on a Claude-managed worktree (`.claude/worktrees/`) вЂ” let the session end naturally; manual removal mid-session breaks the working directory
   238в†’- `git worktree remove` is fine for non-Claude worktrees (e.g. user-created worktrees that need cleanup)
   239в†’
   240в†’## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
   241в†’
   242в†’**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
   243в†’
   244в†’**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
   245в†’
   246в†’1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
   247в†’2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
   248в†’3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
   249в†’4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   250в†’   ```bash
   251в†’   git pull --rebase
   252в†’   bd sync
   253в†’   git push
   254в†’   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   255в†’   ```
   256в†’5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
   257в†’6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
   258в†’7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
   259в†’
   260в†’**CRITICAL RULES:**
   261в†’- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
   262в†’- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
   263в†’- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
   264в†’- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
   265в†’
   266в†’
   267в†’<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   268в†’## Issue Tracking with bd (beads)
   269в†’
   270в†’**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **bd (beads)** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or other tracking methods.
   271в†’
   272в†’### Why bd?
   273в†’
   274в†’- Dependency-aware: Track blockers and relationships between issues
   275в†’- Git-friendly: Auto-syncs to JSONL for version control
   276в†’- Agent-optimized: JSON output, ready work detection, discovered-from links
   277в†’- Prevents duplicate tracking systems and confusion
   278в†’
   279в†’### Quick Start
   280в†’
   281в†’**Check for ready work:**
   282в†’
   283в†’```bash
   284в†’bd ready --json
   285в†’```
   286в†’
   287в†’**Create new issues:**
   288в†’
   289в†’```bash
   290в†’bd create "Issue title" --description="Detailed context" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
   291в†’bd create "Issue title" --description="What this issue is about" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-123 --json
   292в†’```
   293в†’
   294в†’**Claim and update:**
   295в†’
   296в†’```bash
   297в†’bd update bd-42 --status in_progress --json
   298в†’bd update bd-42 --priority 1 --json
   299в†’```
   300в†’
   301в†’**Complete work:**
   302в†’
   303в†’```bash
   304в†’bd close bd-42 --reason "Completed" --json
   305в†’```
   306в†’
   307в†’### Issue Types
   308в†’
   309в†’- `bug` - Something broken
   310в†’- `feature` - New functionality
   311в†’- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
   312в†’- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks
   313в†’- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)
   314в†’
   315в†’### Priorities
   316в†’
   317в†’- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
   318в†’- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
   319в†’- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)
   320в†’- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
   321в†’- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)
   322в†’
   323в†’### Workflow for AI Agents
   324в†’
   325в†’1. **Check ready work**: `bd ready` shows unblocked issues
   326в†’2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --status in_progress`
   327в†’3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
   328в†’4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue:
   329в†’   - `bd create "Found bug" --description="Details about what was found" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
   330в†’5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Done"`
   331в†’
   332в†’### Auto-Sync
   333в†’
   334в†’bd automatically syncs with git:
   335в†’
   336в†’- Exports to `.beads/issues.jsonl` after changes (5s debounce)
   337в†’- Imports from JSONL when newer (e.g., after `git pull`)
   338в†’- No manual export/import needed!
   339в†’
   340в†’### Important Rules
   341в†’
   342в†’- вњ… Use bd for ALL task tracking
   343в†’- вњ… Always use `--json` flag for programmatic use
   344в†’- вњ… Link discovered work with `discovered-from` dependencies
   345в†’- вњ… Check `bd ready` before asking "what should I work on?"
   346в†’- вќЊ Do NOT create markdown TODO lists
   347в†’- вќЊ Do NOT use external issue trackers
   348в†’- вќЊ Do NOT duplicate tracking systems
   349в†’
   350в†’For more details, see README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md.
   351в†’
   352в†’<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   353в†’

1в†’# Contract Gates Specification
     2в†’
     3в†’This document defines how contract failures behave in merge and release
     4в†’workflows, including blocking conditions, waiver paths, and evidence
     5в†’requirements.
     6в†’
     7в†’Related specs: [Search Engine](search-engine.md), [Deployment](deployment.md).
     8в†’
     9в†’---
    10в†’
    11в†’## Overview
    12в†’
    13в†’Contract gates are automated checks that enforce correctness invariants before
    14в†’code enters `main` or ships in a release. They fall into two categories:
    15в†’
    16в†’- **Product contracts** вЂ” verify that user-facing behaviour is correct across
    17в†’  all entry points (e.g., search validation consistency).
    18в†’- **Technical contracts** вЂ” verify that code meets engineering standards
    19в†’  (e.g., lint rules, type safety, coverage thresholds).
    20в†’
    21в†’A failing contract gate means the invariant it protects has been violated.
    22в†’Gates either **block** (hard failure, merge/release cannot proceed) or **warn**
    23в†’(advisory, logged but non-blocking).
    24в†’
    25в†’---
    26в†’
    27в†’## Contract types
    28в†’
    29в†’### Product contracts
    30в†’
    31в†’Product contracts verify that the system behaves correctly from the user's
    32в†’perspective. These are the highest-priority gates because failures here mean
    33в†’users will encounter broken behaviour.
    34в†’
    35в†’| Contract | What it checks | Test command | Tier |
    36в†’|---|---|---|---|
    37в†’| Search contract matrix | Identical validation outcomes across GraphQL, CSV export, and MCP entry points | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
    38в†’| Search field coverage | Every registered field has a `BuildSQL` test case | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
    39в†’| GraphQL schema sync | `web/schema.graphql` matches API SDL | `make web-schema-sync` (check for diff) | Standard |
    40в†’| E2E critical paths | Core user journeys work end-to-end | `make web-test-e2e-critical` | Full |
    41в†’| Fixture compatibility | Fixture data loads against current schema | `make test-fixture` | Full |
    42в†’
    43в†’### Technical contracts
    44в†’
    45в†’Technical contracts enforce code quality and engineering standards. They prevent
    46в†’degradation over time but do not directly correspond to user-facing bugs.
    47в†’
    48в†’| Contract | What it checks | Test command | Tier |
    49в†’|---|---|---|---|
    50в†’| Go unit tests | All unit tests pass (race detector enabled) | `make api-test-unit` | Quick |
    51в†’| TypeScript build | Zero type errors | `pnpm build` (via `make web-build`) | Quick |
    52в†’| ESLint | Zero warnings | `pnpm lint` (via `make web-lint`) | Quick |
    53в†’| Go lint | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) | `make api-lint` | Standard |
    54в†’| Web unit tests | Vitest suite passes, в‰Ґ 80% coverage | `make web-test` | Standard |
    55в†’| Integration tests | Database-backed tests pass | `make api-test` | Full |
    56в†’| Smoke test | Search API responds correctly | `make smoke-test` | Full |
    57в†’
    58в†’---
    59в†’
    60в†’## Test tier mapping
    61в†’
    62в†’Contracts are organized into tiers that balance speed against coverage depth.
    63в†’Each tier includes all contracts from the previous tier.
    64в†’
    65в†’| Tier | Command | Time | External deps | When to run |
    66в†’|---|---|---|---|---|
    67в†’| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10вЂ“20s | None | Every save / pre-push |
    68в†’| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30вЂ“60s | None | Before committing |
    69в†’| Full | `make test-full` | ~2вЂ“5min | DB + services | Before merge to main |
    70в†’
    71в†’---
    72в†’
    73в†’## Merge workflow gates
    74в†’
    75в†’### Blocking (must pass to merge)
    76в†’
    77в†’All contracts in the **Standard** tier block merge. A failure at any level
    78в†’means the branch cannot be merged into `main` until the issue is resolved.
    79в†’
    80в†’Specifically:
    81в†’
    82в†’1. **Go unit tests** вЂ” includes the search contract matrix, field coverage
    83в†’   tests, and all package-level unit tests.
    84в†’2. **TypeScript build** вЂ” catches type errors, missing imports, broken module
    85в†’   resolution.
    86в†’3. **ESLint** вЂ” zero-warning policy; any warning is a failure.
    87в†’4. **Go lint** вЂ” go vet + golangci-lint with gosec security checks.
    88в†’5. **Web unit tests** вЂ” Vitest suite with 80% coverage threshold.
    89в†’
    90в†’### Advisory (warn but do not block)
    91в†’
    92в†’No contracts are currently advisory-only. All defined contracts block merge.
    93в†’If a future contract is added as advisory, it must be explicitly documented
    94в†’here with the rationale for why it does not block.
    95в†’
    96в†’### Pre-merge checklist
    97в†’
    98в†’Before merging any branch into `main`:
    99в†’
   100в†’1. Run `make test-standard` вЂ” all checks must pass.
   101в†’2. For changes touching database code, run `make test-full` to include
   102в†’   integration tests and fixture validation.
   103в†’3. For changes touching frontend user flows, run `make web-test-e2e-critical`.
   104в†’
   105в†’---
   106в†’
   107в†’## Release workflow gates
   108в†’
   109в†’Release gates include everything from merge gates plus additional checks that
   110в†’require a running environment.
   111в†’
   112в†’### Blocking (must pass to release)
   113в†’
   114в†’All **Full** tier contracts must pass before a release:
   115в†’
   116в†’1. All Standard tier contracts (see above).
   117в†’2. **Integration tests** вЂ” database-backed tests with real PostgreSQL.
   118в†’3. **E2E critical paths** вЂ” Playwright tests covering core user journeys.
   119в†’4. **Fixture compatibility** вЂ” fixture data loads cleanly against the current
   120в†’   schema.
   121в†’5. **Smoke test** вЂ” search API returns expected results.
   122в†’
   123в†’### Release verification sequence
   124в†’
   125в†’```bash
   126в†’make test-full          # runs all tiers: quick + standard + full
   127в†’```
   128в†’
   129в†’If `test-full` passes, the release candidate is eligible for deployment.
   130в†’
   131в†’---
   132в†’
   133в†’## Waiver paths
   134в†’
   135в†’A waiver allows merging or releasing despite a contract failure. Waivers are
   136в†’exceptional вЂ” the default is to fix the failure.
   137в†’
   138в†’### When waivers are allowed
   139в†’
   140в†’| Situation | Waiver allowed? | Approval required |
   141в†’|---|---|---|
   142в†’| Test is flaky (not a real failure) | Yes | Owner documents flakiness and files an issue |
   143в†’| External dependency is broken | Yes | Owner documents the dependency issue |
   144в†’| Contract is outdated (tests wrong behaviour) | Yes | Owner updates the contract in the same PR |
   145в†’| Deadline pressure | No | Fix the contract or defer the feature |
   146в†’| "I'll fix it later" | No | Contracts ship with the code |
   147в†’
   148в†’### Waiver process
   149в†’
   150в†’1. **Document the failure**: capture the exact error output and identify the
   151в†’   root cause (flaky test, external issue, or outdated contract).
   152в†’2. **File an issue**: create a beads issue (`bd create`) tracking the fix,
   153в†’   with priority в‰¤ the original contract's priority.
   154в†’3. **Record the waiver**: add a comment to the commit message explaining why
   155в†’   the waiver was granted and referencing the tracking issue.
   156в†’4. **Time-box the fix**: waived contracts must be fixed within 5 business days.
   157в†’   If not fixed, the waiver expires and the contract blocks again.
   158в†’
   159в†’### Waiver commit message format
   160в†’
   161в†’```
   162в†’feat: add new search field for campus size
   163в†’
   164в†’Waiver: TestBuildSQLAllFields skipped вЂ” fixture data does not yet include
   165в†’campus_size column. Tracked in kapow-xyz. Will be resolved when fixture
   166в†’data is regenerated.
   167в†’
   168в†’Co-Authored-By: ...
   169в†’```
   170в†’
   171в†’---
   172в†’
   173в†’## Evidence requirements
   174в†’
   175в†’Every contract must produce evidence that it was evaluated. This ensures
   176в†’that contracts are not silently skipped.
   177в†’
   178в†’### What constitutes evidence
   179в†’
   180в†’| Evidence type | Example | When required |
   181в†’|---|---|---|
   182в†’| Test output | `make test-standard` logs showing pass/fail | Every merge |
   183в†’| CI job log | GitHub Actions run with green/red status | When CI is configured |
   184в†’| Coverage report | Per-package coverage в‰Ґ threshold | Every merge (Go + web) |
   185в†’| Manual verification note | Commit message documenting manual check | Only for waived contracts |
   186в†’
   187в†’### Evidence retention
   188в†’
   189в†’- **CI logs**: retained by the CI provider (GitHub Actions default: 90 days).
   190в†’- **Test output**: not retained beyond the merge вЂ” CI logs serve as the record.
   191в†’- **Waiver issues**: retained in beads until the fix is merged and the issue
   192в†’  is closed.
   193в†’
   194в†’---
   195в†’
   196в†’## Adding a new contract gate
   197в†’
   198в†’When adding a new contract to the gate system:
   199в†’
   200в†’1. **Implement the test** in the appropriate package with clear pass/fail
   201в†’   semantics.
   202в†’2. **Assign a tier** (Quick, Standard, or Full) based on its dependencies
   203в†’   and execution time.
   204в†’3. **Add it to the Makefile** target for its tier, or verify it is already
   205в†’   included via an existing target.
   206в†’4. **Document it** by adding a row to the appropriate table in this spec
   207в†’   (product or technical contracts).
   208в†’5. **Set blocking behaviour**: new contracts default to blocking. If advisory,
   209в†’   document the rationale in this spec.
   210в†’

1в†’# Kapow вЂ” College Power Search: Product Specification
     2в†’
     3в†’## 1. Product Overview
     4в†’
     5в†’**Kapow (College Power Search)** is a web application that provides a comprehensive, faceted search engine for US colleges and universities. It aggregates data from 12 independent sources into a single searchable interface, enabling users to filter, sort, compare, and export institution data across 48 searchable dimensions.
     6в†’
     7в†’### Target Users
     8в†’
     9в†’- **Prospective students** researching colleges that fit their academic, financial, geographic, and lifestyle preferences
    10в†’- **Parents and families** comparing cost, outcomes, and value across institutions
    11в†’- **High school counselors** building filtered shortlists and exporting data for students
    12в†’- **AI assistants** querying college data programmatically via the MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint
    13в†’
    14в†’### Core Value Proposition
    15в†’
    16в†’College Power Search consolidates data that is otherwise scattered across dozens of websites вЂ” federal statistics, multiple ranking publications, athletic conferences, climate data, partisan lean scores, and application platform listings вЂ” into a single interface with real-time faceted filtering. Users can combine any number of criteria simultaneously, view results in four different formats, create custom ranking blends from multiple sources, and export their findings as CSV or PDF.
    17в†’
    18в†’---
    19в†’
    20в†’## 2. Feature Inventory
    21в†’
    22в†’### 2.1 Faceted Search Engine
    23в†’
    24в†’The search engine exposes 48 filterable fields organized into 11 active themes (with 7 additional theme slots reserved for future expansion). Every filter is applied server-side as a parameterized SQL WHERE clause against a denormalized materialized view (`dataset_merged`), ensuring fast queries across the full dataset.
    25в†’
    26в†’**Active themes and their fields:**
    27в†’
    28в†’| Theme | Fields | Description |
    29в†’|---|---|---|
    30в†’| Core | Institution Name (full-text), Unit ID | Name search across institution name, city, and state |
    31в†’| Location | City, State, Geographic Proximity, Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance | Where the school is and what kind of setting it occupies |
    32в†’| Academics | Institution Type, Highest Degree, Enrollment, Graduate Enrollment, Student-Faculty Ratio, Full-Time Faculty Rate, Gender (coed/single-sex), Online Only, Carnegie Classification | Academic profile and size |
    33в†’| Cost & Financial Aid | Avg Net Price, In-State Tuition, Out-of-State Tuition, Room & Board, Net Price (low-income), Pell Grant Rate, Federal Loan Rate, Endowment | What it costs and how it is funded |
    34в†’| Outcomes | Admission Rate, Graduation Rate, Retention Rate, Transfer Rate | Student success metrics |
    35в†’| Rankings | US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly National, Washington Monthly Liberal Arts, Niche, Money Score | Rankings from 6 publications (7 lists) |
    36в†’| Admissions | Application Platforms (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | How to apply |
    37в†’| Athletics | NCAA Division, NCAA Conference | Athletic conference and competition level |
    38в†’| Fields of Study | CIP-coded program offerings | What majors and programs are available |
    39в†’| Cultural Environment | Partisan Direction, Partisan Score, Congressional District | Political leaning of the surrounding area |
    40в†’| Weather | Koppen Climate Group, Koppen Temperature Subtype, Koppen Precipitation Subtype | Climate classification of the campus location |
    41в†’
    42в†’**Filter types supported:**
    43в†’
    44в†’| Filter Type | Behavior | Example |
    45в†’|---|---|---|
    46в†’| Full-text search | PostgreSQL `tsvector` matching | "MIT" matches Massachusetts Institute of Technology |
    47в†’| Substring match | Case-insensitive partial match | "Arbor" matches Ann Arbor |
    48в†’| Single enum | Exact match from a fixed option list | Institution Type = "Public" |
    49в†’| Multi enum | Match any or all from a set | State in [CA, NY, MA] |
    50в†’| Numeric range | Min/max bounds on a numeric value | Tuition between $5,000 and $20,000 |
    51в†’| Exact string | Case-insensitive exact match | Metro Area = "Boston" |
    52в†’| Exact numeric | Exact integer match | Carnegie Classification = 15 |
    53в†’| Geographic proximity | PostGIS radius search | Within 50 miles of a given lat/lng |
    54в†’| Ranking | Numeric range on ranking position | US News rank 1-25 |
    55в†’
    56в†’### 2.2 Four Result Views
    57в†’
    58в†’Users can switch between four ways to view search results:
    59в†’
    60в†’| View | Description |
    61в†’|---|---|
    62в†’| **Card** (default) | Responsive grid of summary cards showing key stats per institution |
    63в†’| **List** | Compact row-based list emphasizing quick scanning |
    64в†’| **Table** | Full data table with sortable columns, customizable column visibility, and drag-to-reorder |
    65в†’| **Map** | Leaflet-based map with institution markers; supports click-to-detail |
    66в†’
    67в†’The selected view mode persists in `localStorage` and can also be set via URL parameter (`?view=table`).
    68в†’
    69в†’### 2.3 Custom Ranking Blends
    70в†’
    71в†’Users can create weighted composite rankings by assigning weights to any combination of the 7 ranking fields. When a blend is active, results are sorted by the computed blend score rather than by a single column. Blend weights are serialized in the URL (`?blend=...`) so blended searches can be shared via link.
    72в†’
    73в†’### 2.4 Sorting and Pagination
    74в†’
    75в†’- Any field marked as `sortable` can be used as the sort column (ascending or descending)
    76в†’- When a user location is set, results can be sorted by distance
    77в†’- Pagination supports configurable page sizes (5, 10, 20, 50, 100) with a maximum of 100 results per search
    78в†’- All sort and pagination state is encoded in URL parameters for shareability
    79в†’
    80в†’### 2.5 Column Customization (Table View)
    81в†’
    82в†’In table view, users can:
    83в†’- Show or hide any output field using a column picker
    84в†’- Reorder columns via drag-and-drop or up/down controls
    85в†’- Column visibility and order persist in `localStorage`
    86в†’- Default-visible columns are determined by field metadata from the API
    87в†’
    88в†’### 2.6 Institution Detail
    89в†’
    90в†’Clicking any institution in any view opens a detail drawer/modal showing expanded information including:
    91в†’- Core identity (name, city, state, coordinates)
    92в†’- Institution type and enrollment
    93в†’- Admission and graduation rates
    94в†’- Tuition (in-state and out-of-state) and average net price
    95в†’- Retention rate and student-faculty ratio
    96в†’
    97в†’### 2.7 Similar Institutions
    98в†’
    99в†’The API provides a `similarInstitutions` query that, given a specific institution, returns other schools with similar profiles based on type, enrollment, admission rate, graduation rate, and tuition. Results include a similarity distance score.
   100в†’
   101в†’### 2.8 CSV and PDF Export
   102в†’
   103в†’- **CSV Export**: Server-side export via `/api/export/csv` that respects active filters, sort order, and user location. Downloads directly as a file. Includes all exportable fields.
   104в†’- **PDF Export**: Client-side PDF generation using the current result set, active output fields, and active filter labels. Includes data source attribution.
   105в†’
   106в†’### 2.9 Geographic Features
   107в†’
   108в†’- **Proximity search**: PostGIS-powered `ST_DWithin` queries with configurable radius (up to ~310 miles / 500 km)
   109в†’- **Distance calculation**: When a user location is set, each result includes its distance from that location
   110в†’- **My Price**: When a user's home state is known, the system returns in-state tuition for public schools in that state and out-of-state tuition otherwise
   111в†’- **Map view**: Interactive Leaflet map with markers for all results in the current page
   112в†’- **ZIP code resolution**: The `resolveZip` query resolves a US ZIP code to latitude/longitude and state, used by the `LocationInput` component for location-by-ZIP entry (queries `location.zip_coordinates`)
   113в†’
   114в†’### 2.10 Authentication and User Preferences
   115в†’
   116в†’- **Authentication**: Supabase-based authentication supporting Google, GitHub, and email sign-in via OAuth
   117в†’- **User profile**: The `me` query returns the authenticated user's ID, email, display name, avatar, and auth provider
   118в†’- **Preferences**: Authenticated users can save a home location (latitude, longitude, state) that automatically populates the user location for distance calculations and My Price computation
   119в†’
   120в†’### 2.11 Sample Searches
   121в†’
   122в†’The home page features a carousel of pre-built sample searches that demonstrate the system's filtering capabilities:
   123в†’
   124в†’| Sample | Filters Applied |
   125в†’|---|---|
   126в†’| Top 25 National | US News National rank <= 25 |
   127в†’| Affordable in California | State = CA, net price <= $20,000 |
   128в†’| D1 Small Schools | NCAA Division I, enrollment <= 5,000 |
   129в†’| Selective & Affordable | Admission rate <= 30%, net price <= $20,000 |
   130в†’| D3 New England | NCAA Division III, New England region |
   131в†’| Sunny & Affordable | Dry (Koppen B) climate, net price <= $15,000 |
   132в†’
   133в†’Each sample search shows a live preview of matching institutions before the user navigates to the full search page.
   134в†’
   135в†’### 2.12 MCP (Model Context Protocol) Endpoint
   136в†’
   137в†’The application exposes a WebMCP interface that allows AI clients (such as Claude) to programmatically search the college database. Two tools are registered:
   138в†’
   139в†’| Tool | Description |
   140в†’|---|---|
   141в†’| `search_colleges` | Execute a filtered search with field/value pairs, limit, and offset |
   142в†’| `get_search_fields` | Retrieve the full field descriptor catalog including types and options |
   143в†’
   144в†’### 2.13 Data Source Attribution
   145в†’
   146в†’Every search field is linked to its originating data source. The UI can display source badges and tooltips, and the `dataSources` GraphQL query returns the full catalog with names, short names, URLs, and descriptions. This attribution carries through to PDF exports.
   147в†’
   148в†’---
   149в†’
   150в†’## 3. Pages
   151в†’
   152в†’| Route | Page | Description |
   153в†’|---|---|---|
   154в†’| `/` | Home | Landing page with hero search form (name, state, ranking, major), sample search carousel, and feature highlights |
   155в†’| `/search` | Search | Main search interface with collapsible filter sidebar (desktop: fixed 280px sidebar; mobile: toggle-to-show), results area with view mode toggle, export buttons, pagination, and institution detail drawer |
   156в†’| `/about` | About | Information page about the application |
   157в†’| `/auth/callback` | Auth Callback | OAuth redirect handler for Supabase authentication |
   158в†’
   159в†’All pages share a common `Header` (with navigation and auth controls) and `Footer`.
   160в†’
   161в†’---
   162в†’
   163в†’## 4. User Stories
   164в†’
   165в†’### Student
   166в†’
   167в†’- As a student, I want to search for colleges by name so I can quickly find a specific school.
   168в†’- As a student, I want to filter by state, cost, and admission rate simultaneously so I can find affordable schools I have a realistic chance of attending.
   169в†’- As a student, I want to filter by fields of study so I can find schools that offer my intended major.
   170в†’- As a student, I want to see how far each school is from my home so I can gauge travel time.
   171в†’- As a student, I want to view results on a map so I can see the geographic distribution of matching schools.
   172в†’- As a student, I want to create a custom ranking blend weighting cost and graduation rate so I can find the best value schools by my own criteria.
   173в†’
   174в†’### Parent
   175в†’
   176в†’- As a parent, I want to compare in-state and out-of-state tuition so I can understand the true cost.
   177в†’- As a parent, I want to see my personalized net price (My Price) so I know what public schools in my state would actually cost.
   178в†’- As a parent, I want to sort by graduation rate so I can prioritize schools where students succeed.
   179в†’- As a parent, I want to export the shortlist as a PDF so I can share it with my student.
   180в†’
   181в†’### Counselor
   182в†’
   183в†’- As a counselor, I want to export a filtered list as CSV so I can analyze it in a spreadsheet.
   184в†’- As a counselor, I want to use the table view with custom columns so I can compare specific metrics across many schools.
   185в†’- As a counselor, I want to share a search via URL so I can send a pre-filtered view to a student or family.
   186в†’- As a counselor, I want to use sample searches as starting points for common student profiles.
   187в†’
   188в†’### Authenticated User
   189в†’
   190в†’- As a returning user, I want to save my home location so distance and My Price are calculated automatically on every visit.
   191в†’- As a user, I want my column layout and view mode preferences to persist between sessions.
   192в†’
   193в†’### AI Client
   194в†’
   195в†’- As an AI assistant, I want to query available search fields so I can understand what filters exist.
   196в†’- As an AI assistant, I want to search colleges with structured filters so I can answer user questions about college options.
   197в†’

   205в†’| 2 | U.S. News & World Report вЂ” National Universities | US News | National university rankings | usnews.com |
   206в†’| 3 | U.S. News & World Report вЂ” Liberal Arts Colleges | US News | Liberal arts college rankings | usnews.com |
   207в†’| 4 | Forbes America's Top Colleges | Forbes | Overall college rankings | forbes.com |
   208в†’| 5 | Washington Monthly College Rankings | Wash Monthly | Rankings emphasizing social mobility, research, and public service (national + liberal arts) | washingtonmonthly.com |
   209в†’| 6 | Niche Best Colleges | Niche | Rankings based on academics, value, and student reviews | niche.com |
   210в†’| 7 | Money Magazine Best Colleges | Money | College value scores balancing quality, affordability, and outcomes | money.com |

   216в†’

   218в†’
   219в†’## 6. Architecture Overview
   220в†’
   221в†’Kapow is a monorepo with a Go API backend, a React frontend, and a PostgreSQL
   222в†’database. For detailed architecture documentation, see:
   223в†’
   224в†’- **[Frontend Architecture](frontend-architecture.md)** вЂ” tech stack, components, state management, GraphQL integration
   225в†’- **[GraphQL API](graphql-api.md)** вЂ” queries, mutations, types, request lifecycle
   226в†’- **[Search Engine](search-engine.md)** вЂ” field types, SQL generation, themes, data sources
   227в†’- **[Data Model](data-model.md)** вЂ” schemas, tables, relationships, migrations
   228в†’- **[Authentication](authentication.md)** вЂ” JWT flow, middleware, Supabase integration
   229в†’- **[Deployment](deployment.md)** вЂ” Docker images, Compose, env vars, startup sequence

1в†’# GraphQL API Specification
     2в†’
     3в†’Kapow exposes a single GraphQL endpoint at `/graphql` (POST and GET).
     4в†’This document describes the complete API contract that clients need to
     5в†’consume the service. For search field details, see
     6в†’[search-engine.md](search-engine.md). For authentication flow, see
     7в†’[authentication.md](authentication.md).
     8в†’
     9в†’---
    10в†’
    11в†’## Endpoint
    12в†’
    13в†’```
    14в†’POST /graphql
    15в†’GET  /graphql   (query via query-string вЂ” primarily for GraphiQL)
    16в†’```
    17в†’
    18в†’**Content-Type**: `application/json`
    19в†’
    20в†’**Authentication**: OptionalAuth. Anonymous requests are accepted and can
    21в†’execute public queries. If a valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header is
    22в†’present, the user is provisioned (team + membership created) and claims are
    23в†’available to resolvers. Invalid or malformed tokens are silently ignored;
    24в†’the request proceeds as unauthenticated.
    25в†’
    26в†’---
    27в†’
    28в†’## Request Lifecycle
    29в†’
    30в†’A GraphQL request to `/graphql` traverses the following layers:
    31в†’
    32в†’1. **Global middleware** (applied to all routes, in order):
    33в†’   - `OTel` (conditional) вЂ” when `OTEL_ENABLED=true`, `otelhttp` middleware records
    34в†’     HTTP metrics (duration, count by method/status) and creates a trace span per request
    35в†’   - `RequestID` вЂ” generates or propagates `X-Request-ID`
    36в†’   - `Logging` вЂ” structured slog request/response logging
    37в†’   - `Recovery` вЂ” panic recovery в†’ 500 response
    38в†’   - `HTTPDetail` вЂ” optional HTTP debug header/body logging
    39в†’   - `RealIP` (chi) вЂ” resolves client IP from proxy headers
    40в†’   - `StripSlashes` (chi) вЂ” normalizes trailing slashes
    41в†’
    42в†’2. **Route middleware** (applied to `/graphql` group):
    43в†’   - `OptionalAuth` вЂ” if `Authorization: Bearer <token>` is present and valid,
    44в†’     stores `*auth.Claims` in context; if absent, passes through anonymously;
    45в†’     if present and invalid, returns 401
    46в†’   - `EnsureUser` вЂ” if claims exist, idempotently creates a personal team +
    47в†’     team membership row via `user.Service.EnsureUser`
    48в†’
    49в†’3. **gqlgen handler** вЂ” parses the GraphQL operation, validates it against the
    50в†’   schema, and dispatches to the appropriate resolver method on the `Resolver`
    51в†’   struct (which holds `DB`, `Search` registry, and `Preference` service)
    52в†’
    53в†’4. **Resolver** вЂ” executes business logic:
    54в†’   - Search queries call `ValidateRequest()` в†’ `BuildQuery()` / `BuildCountQuery()`
    55в†’   - SQL is executed via `pgxpool.Pool.Query()` with parameterized placeholders
    56в†’   - Results are serialized into the GraphQL response types
    57в†’
    58в†’5. **Response** вЂ” gqlgen serializes the response as JSON and returns it with
    59в†’   HTTP 200 (even for GraphQL-level errors; see [Error Handling](#error-handling))
    60в†’
    61в†’For `/mcp/*` routes, the middleware stack replaces `OptionalAuth` with
    62в†’mandatory `Auth` вЂ” unauthenticated requests receive a 401 with a
    63в†’`WWW-Authenticate` header. See [authentication.md](authentication.md) for the
    64в†’complete route-level auth table.
    65в†’
    66в†’---
    67в†’
    68в†’## Queries
    69в†’
    70в†’### echo
    71в†’
    72в†’Health-check / connectivity test.
    73в†’
    74в†’```graphql
    75в†’query {
    76в†’  echo(message: "hello") {
    77в†’    message
    78в†’    receivedAt
    79в†’  }
    80в†’}
    81в†’```
    82в†’
    83в†’| Argument | Type | Required | Description |
    84в†’|----------|------|----------|-------------|
    85в†’| `message` | `String!` | yes | Arbitrary string echoed back |
    86в†’
    87в†’**Returns**: `EchoResponse!`
    88в†’
    89в†’| Field | Type | Description |
    90в†’|-------|------|-------------|
    91в†’| `message` | `String!` | The input message, echoed verbatim |
    92в†’| `receivedAt` | `String!` | Server timestamp (ISO 8601) |
    93в†’
    94в†’**Auth**: not required.
    95в†’
    96в†’---
    97в†’
    98в†’### me
    99в†’
   100в†’Returns the authenticated user's profile, derived from JWT claims.
   101в†’
   102в†’```graphql
   103в†’query {
   104в†’  me {
   105в†’    id
   106в†’    email
   107в†’    fullName
   108в†’    avatarUrl
   109в†’    provider
   110в†’  }
   111в†’}
   112в†’```
   113в†’
   114в†’**Returns**: `User` (nullable). Returns `null` when no valid token is present.
   115в†’
   116в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   117в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   118в†’| `id` | `ID!` | Supabase user UUID (`sub` claim) |
   119в†’| `email` | `String!` | User email |
   120в†’| `fullName` | `String` | Display name from identity provider |
   121в†’| `avatarUrl` | `String` | Profile image URL |
   122в†’| `provider` | `String` | Auth provider (e.g. `google`, `github`) |
   123в†’
   124в†’**Auth**: not required, but returns `null` without a valid token.

   127в†’
   128в†’### search
   129в†’
   130в†’Primary search query. Accepts filters, pagination, sorting, user location
   131в†’(for distance calculations), and optional ranking blend weights.
   132в†’
   133в†’```graphql
   134в†’query Search($filters: [FilterInput!], $limit: Int, $offset: Int) {
   135в†’  search(filters: $filters, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
   136в†’    total
   137в†’    limit
   138в†’    offset
   139в†’    results {
   140в†’      unitID
   141в†’      name
   142в†’      city
   143в†’      state
   144в†’      distance
   145в†’      myPrice
   146в†’      blendScore
   147в†’      admissions { applicationPlatforms }
   148в†’      rankings { usNewsNational forbes }
   149в†’    }
   150в†’  }
   151в†’}
   152в†’```
   153в†’
   154в†’| Argument | Type | Required | Description |
   155в†’|----------|------|----------|-------------|
   156в†’| `filters` | `[FilterInput!]` | no | Array of field-name/value filter pairs |
   157в†’| `limit` | `Int` | no | Page size (server default applies if omitted) |
   158в†’| `offset` | `Int` | no | Number of results to skip |
   159в†’| `sort` | `SortInput` | no | Sort field and direction |
   160в†’| `userLocation` | `UserLocation` | no | User coordinates for distance calculation |
   161в†’| `rankingBlend` | `[RankingBlendWeight!]` | no | Custom ranking blend weights |
   162в†’
   163в†’**Returns**: `SearchResponse!`
   164в†’
   165в†’**Auth**: not required.
   166в†’
   167в†’#### SearchResponse
   168в†’
   169в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   170в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   171в†’| `results` | `[SearchInstitution!]!` | Matching institutions for the current page |
   172в†’| `total` | `Int!` | Total number of matching institutions |
   173в†’| `limit` | `Int!` | Page size used |
   174в†’| `offset` | `Int!` | Offset used |
   175в†’
   176в†’#### SearchInstitution
   177в†’
   178в†’Top-level scalar fields:
   179в†’
   180в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   181в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   182в†’| `unitID` | `String!` | IPEDS Unit ID |
   183в†’| `name` | `String!` | Institution name |
   184в†’| `city` | `String!` | City |
   185в†’| `state` | `String!` | State abbreviation |
   186в†’| `latitude` | `Float` | Latitude |
   187в†’| `longitude` | `Float` | Longitude |
   188в†’| `distance` | `Float` | Miles from `userLocation` (null if not provided) |
   189в†’| `myPrice` | `Int` | Estimated net price based on user's state residency |
   190в†’| `blendScore` | `Float` | Composite ranking score (null if no `rankingBlend`) |
   191в†’
   192в†’Nested objects:
   193в†’
   194в†’| Field | Type | Key sub-fields |
   195в†’|-------|------|----------------|
   196в†’| `locationDetails` | `InstitutionLocation` | locale, region, metroArea, metroDistance |
   197в†’| `academics` | `InstitutionAcademics` | institutionType, highestDegree, enrollment, gradEnrollment, studentFacultyRatio, fullTimeFacultyRate, gender, onlineOnly |
   198в†’| `cost` | `InstitutionCost` | avgNetPrice, tuitionInState, tuitionOutOfState, roomAndBoard, avgNetPriceLowIncome, pellGrantRate, federalLoanRate, endowment |
   199в†’| `outcomes` | `InstitutionOutcomes` | admissionRate, graduationRate, retentionRate, transferRate |
   200в†’| `rankings` | `InstitutionRankings` | usNewsNational, usNewsLiberalArts, forbes, washingtonMonthlyNational, washingtonMonthlyLiberalArts, niche, moneyScore |
   201в†’| `admissions` | `InstitutionAdmissions` | applicationPlatforms |
   202в†’| `athletics` | `InstitutionAthletics` | ncaaDivision, ncaaConference |
   203в†’| `climate` | `InstitutionClimate` | koppenClimate, koppenTemperature, koppenPrecipitation |
   204в†’| `culture` | `InstitutionCulture` | partisanDirection, partisanScore |
   205в†’

   297в†’
   298в†’### searchFields
   299в†’
   300в†’Returns all available search/filter fields with metadata describing their type,
   301в†’validation constraints, and UI grouping. Clients use this to dynamically render
   302в†’filter controls. See [search-engine.md](search-engine.md) for field details.
   303в†’
   304в†’```graphql
   305в†’query {
   306в†’  searchFields {
   307в†’    name
   308в†’    label
   309в†’    description
   310в†’    theme
   311в†’    visibility
   312в†’    searchability
   313в†’    ... on ChoiceSearchField { options { value displayName } }
   314в†’    ... on NumericalRangeSearchField { min max step unit }
   315в†’    ... on GeoSearchField { defaultLatitude defaultLongitude maxRadiusMiles }
   316в†’  }
   317в†’}
   318в†’```
   319в†’
   320в†’**Returns**: `[SearchField!]!`
   321в†’
   322в†’`SearchField` is an interface implemented by six concrete types. All share
   323в†’these base fields:
   324в†’
   325в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   326в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   327в†’| `name` | `String!` | Filter key used in `FilterInput.field` |
   328в†’| `label` | `String!` | Human-readable display label |
   329в†’| `description` | `String!` | Help text |
   330в†’| `theme` | `Theme!` | UI grouping category |
   331в†’| `visibility` | `FieldVisibility!` | Display visibility level |
   332в†’| `searchability` | `FieldSearchability!` | Whether the field is filterable |
   333в†’| `exportable` | `Boolean!` | Whether the field can be exported |
   334в†’| `dataSourceID` | `String` | ID of the originating data source |
   335в†’
   336в†’#### SearchField implementations
   337в†’
   338в†’| Type | Extra fields | Filter value format |
   339в†’|------|-------------|---------------------|
   340в†’| `TextSearchField` | `minLength`, `maxLength`, `pattern` | `"search term"` (string) |
   341в†’| `BinarySearchField` | (none) | `"1"` (string) |
   342в†’| `ChoiceSearchField` | `options`, `minSelections`, `maxSelections` | `["val1", "val2"]` (string array) |
   343в†’| `NumericalSearchField` | `min`, `max`, `step`, `unit`, `defaultValue` | `42` (number) |
   344в†’| `NumericalRangeSearchField` | `min`, `max`, `step`, `unit`, `defaultLower`, `defaultUpper`, `minSpan`, `maxSpan` | `[10, 90]` (two-element number array) |
   345в†’| `GeoSearchField` | `defaultLatitude`, `defaultLongitude`, `maxRadiusMiles` | `{"latitude": 40.7, "longitude": -74.0, "radiusMiles": 50}` |
   346в†’
   347в†’**Auth**: not required.
   348в†’

   484в†’## Mutations
   485в†’
   486в†’### updatePreferences
   487в†’
   488в†’Replaces the user's preferences with the provided input. All fields are
   489в†’optional; omitted fields are set to null.
   490в†’
   491в†’```graphql
   492в†’mutation {
   493в†’  updatePreferences(input: {
   494в†’    homeLatitude: 42.3601
   495в†’    homeLongitude: -71.0589
   496в†’    homeState: "MA"
   497в†’  }) {
   498в†’    homeLatitude
   499в†’    homeLongitude
   500в†’    homeState
   501в†’  }
   502в†’}
   503в†’```
   504в†’
   505в†’| Argument | Type | Required | Description |
   506в†’|----------|------|----------|-------------|
   507в†’| `input` | `UpdatePreferencesInput!` | yes | New preferences |
   508в†’
   509в†’**UpdatePreferencesInput**:
   510в†’
   511в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   512в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   513в†’| `homeLatitude` | `Float` | Home latitude |
   514в†’| `homeLongitude` | `Float` | Home longitude |
   515в†’| `homeState` | `String` | Home state abbreviation |
   516в†’

   518в†’

   522в†’
   523в†’## Input Types
   524в†’
   525в†’### FilterInput
   526в†’
   527в†’Used in the `search` query to apply filters.
   528в†’
   529в†’```json
   530в†’{ "field": "state", "value": ["CA", "NY"] }
   531в†’```
   532в†’
   533в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   534в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   535в†’| `field` | `SearchFieldName!` | Field identifier (enum) |
   536в†’| `value` | `FilterValue!` | Filter value (type depends on field) |
   537в†’
   538в†’`FilterValue` is a custom scalar backed by arbitrary JSON. The expected shape
   539в†’depends on the field type -- see the "Filter value format" column in the
   540в†’[SearchField implementations](#searchfield-implementations) table above.
   541в†’
   542в†’### SortInput
   543в†’
   544в†’```json
   545в†’{ "field": "name", "direction": "ASC" }
   546в†’```
   547в†’
   548в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   549в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   550в†’| `field` | `SearchFieldName!` | Field to sort by |
   551в†’| `direction` | `SortDirection` | `ASC` (default) or `DESC` |
   552в†’
   553в†’### UserLocation
   554в†’
   555в†’Provides the user's coordinates for distance calculation and in-state
   556в†’tuition pricing.
   557в†’
   558в†’```json
   559в†’{ "latitude": 40.7128, "longitude": -74.006, "state": "NY" }
   560в†’```
   561в†’
   562в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   563в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   564в†’| `latitude` | `Float!` | User latitude |
   565в†’| `longitude` | `Float!` | User longitude |
   566в†’| `state` | `String` | User's home state (for `myPrice` calculation) |
   567в†’
   568в†’### RankingBlendWeight
   569в†’
   570в†’Defines a weight for a ranking field in a custom composite score.
   571в†’
   572в†’```json
   573в†’{ "field": "rank_us_news_national", "weight": 0.5 }
   574в†’```
   575в†’
   576в†’| Field | Type | Description |
   577в†’|-------|------|-------------|
   578в†’| `field` | `SearchFieldName!` | A ranking-type field name |
   579в†’| `weight` | `Float!` | Weight (0.0 -- 1.0); weights are normalized server-side |
   580в†’
   581в†’---
   582в†’
   583в†’## Enums
   584в†’
   585в†’### Theme
   586в†’
   587в†’Groups search fields by category for UI organization.
   588в†’
   589в†’```
   590в†’CORE, LOCATION, ACADEMICS, COST, OUTCOMES, RANKING, ADMISSIONS,
   591в†’ATHLETICS, FIELDS_OF_STUDY, CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT, WEATHER,
   592в†’PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT, DEMOGRAPHICS, REPUTATION, SAFETY,
   593в†’STUDENT_LIFE, METADATA, OTHER
   594в†’```
   595в†’
   596в†’### SearchFieldName
   597в†’
   598в†’All 48 registered search field identifiers. Auto-generated from the field
   599в†’registry -- see `api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql` for the full list.
   600в†’Notable fields: `name`, `state`, `location` (geo), `enrollment`,
   601в†’`admission_rate`, `graduation_rate`, `tuition_in_state`, `distance`,
   602в†’`rank_us_news_national`, `fields_of_study`.
   603в†’
   604в†’### FieldVisibility
   605в†’
   606в†’Controls whether a field is shown in the UI.
   607в†’
   608в†’| Value | Description |
   609в†’|-------|-------------|
   610в†’| `VISIBLE` | Shown to all users |
   611в†’| `RESTRICTED` | Shown only to authorized users |
   612в†’| `HIDDEN` | Not shown in the UI |
   613в†’
   614в†’> **Note**: `RESTRICTED` is defined in the enum but **not currently enforced**.
   615в†’> No fields use it, and no auth checks exist in resolvers or validation to
   616в†’> filter fields by user role. It is a placeholder for future role-based access.
   617в†’
   618в†’### FieldSearchability
   619в†’
   620в†’Controls whether a field can be used as a filter.
   621в†’
   622в†’| Value | Description |
   623в†’|-------|-------------|
   624в†’| `SEARCHABLE` | Available as a filter |
   625в†’| `RESTRICTED` | Filter restricted to authorized users |
   626в†’| `NOT_SEARCHABLE` | Output-only; cannot be used as a filter |
   627в†’
   628в†’> **Note**: `RESTRICTED` is defined but not enforced вЂ” same caveat as
   629в†’> `FieldVisibility.RESTRICTED` above.
   630в†’
   631в†’### SortDirection
   632в†’
   633в†’| Value | Description |
   634в†’|-------|-------------|
   635в†’| `ASC` | Ascending |
   636в†’| `DESC` | Descending |
   637в†’
   638в†’### SearchFieldUnit
   639в†’
   640в†’Display format hint for numerical fields.
   641в†’
   642в†’```
   643в†’DOLLARS, PERCENTAGE, MILES, HOURS, MINUTES
   644в†’```
   645в†’
   646в†’---
   647в†’
   648в†’## Error Handling
   649в†’
   650в†’All resolver errors are returned as plain strings in `errors[].message`. The
   651в†’API does **not** use `extensions.code` вЂ” there are no structured error codes.
   652в†’The HTTP status is always **200** for GraphQL-level errors; **401** is only
   653в†’returned by middleware (e.g., invalid token on `/mcp/*`).
   654в†’
   655в†’### Error response format
   656в†’
   657в†’```json
   658в†’{
   659в†’  "data": null,
   660в†’  "errors": [
   661в†’    {
   662в†’      "message": "preferences: authentication required",
   663в†’      "path": ["preferences"]
   664в†’    }
   665в†’  ]
   666в†’}
   667в†’```
   668в†’
   669в†’### Error categories
   670в†’
   671в†’| Category | Message pattern | Example |
   672в†’|----------|----------------|---------|
   673в†’| Auth required | `"<resolver>: authentication required"` | `"preferences: authentication required"`, `"updatePreferences: authentication required"` |
   674в†’| Search validation | `"search: validation: field \"<name>\": <detail>"` | `"search: validation: field \"enrollment\": min must be <= max"` |
   675в†’| Search validation (global) | `"search: validation: <detail>"` | `"search: validation: limit must not exceed 100"` |
   676в†’| Database / internal | `"search: count query: <detail>"`, `"search: query: <detail>"` | `"search: query: ERROR: ..."` |
   677в†’| Not found | `null` data field | `institution(unitID: "invalid")` returns `{"data": {"institution": null}}` |
   678в†’
   679в†’### Validation errors
   680в†’
   681в†’`ValidateRequest()` in the search engine collects all validation errors without
   682в†’short-circuiting. Each error has a `Field` (the filter key or a sentinel like
   683в†’`_limit`, `_offset`) and a `Message`. The resolver surfaces only the **first**
   684в†’validation error as a GraphQL error string in the format shown above.
   685в†’
   686в†’---
   687в†’
   688в†’## Usage Examples
   689в†’
   690в†’### Filtered search with pagination
   691в†’
   692в†’```graphql
   693в†’query FilteredSearch {
   694в†’  search(
   695в†’    filters: [
   696в†’      { field: state, value: ["CA", "NY", "MA"] }
   697в†’      { field: enrollment, value: [1000, 10000] }
   698в†’      { field: admission_rate, value: [0, 0.3] }
   699в†’    ]
   700в†’    limit: 25
   701в†’    offset: 0
   702в†’    sort: { field: name, direction: ASC }
   703в†’    userLocation: { latitude: 40.7128, longitude: -74.006, state: "NY" }
   704в†’  ) {
   705в†’    total
   706в†’    results {
   707в†’      unitID
   708в†’      name
   709в†’      state
   710в†’      distance
   711в†’      myPrice
   712в†’      outcomes { admissionRate graduationRate }
   713в†’      cost { avgNetPrice tuitionInState tuitionOutOfState }
   714в†’      rankings { usNewsNational forbes }
   715в†’    }
   716в†’  }
   717в†’}
   718в†’```
   719в†’
   720в†’### Custom ranking blend
   721в†’
   722в†’```graphql
   723в†’query BlendedSearch {
   724в†’  search(
   725в†’    rankingBlend: [
   726в†’      { field: rank_us_news_national, weight: 0.4 }
   727в†’      { field: rank_forbes, weight: 0.3 }
   728в†’      { field: score_money, weight: 0.3 }
   729в†’    ]
   730в†’    limit: 20
   731в†’  ) {
   732в†’    results {
   733в†’      name
   734в†’      blendScore
   735в†’      rankings { usNewsNational forbes moneyScore }
   736в†’    }
   737в†’  }
   738в†’}
   739в†’```
   740в†’
   741в†’### Dynamic filter UI bootstrap
   742в†’
   743в†’```graphql
   744в†’query BootstrapFilters {
   745в†’  searchFields {
   746в†’    name
   747в†’    label
   748в†’    description
   749в†’    theme
   750в†’    visibility
   751в†’    searchability
   752в†’    ... on TextSearchField { minLength maxLength }
   753в†’    ... on ChoiceSearchField {
   754в†’      options { value displayName }
   755в†’      minSelections
   756в†’      maxSelections
   757в†’    }
   758в†’    ... on NumericalRangeSearchField {
   759в†’      min max step unit defaultLower defaultUpper
   760в†’    }
   761в†’    ... on GeoSearchField {
   762в†’      defaultLatitude defaultLongitude maxRadiusMiles
   763в†’    }
   764в†’  }
   765в†’  searchOutputFields {
   766в†’    name label defaultVisible sortable unit outputKey
   767в†’  }
   768в†’  dataSources {
   769в†’    id name url fields
   770в†’  }
   771в†’}
   772в†’```

1в†’# Data Model Specification
     2в†’
     3в†’Comprehensive reference for the Kapow database schema, table definitions,
     4в†’relationships, indexes, and data flow.
     5в†’
     6в†’---
     7в†’
     8в†’## Database
     9в†’
    10в†’- **Engine**: PostgreSQL 15+ with PostGIS extension
    11в†’- **Driver**: `jackc/pgx/v5` + `pgxpool` (context-aware, async connection pooling)
    12в†’- **Migrations**: `pressly/goose/v3`, embedded in the API binary via `api/migrations/migrations.go`
    13в†’- **SRID**: 4326 (WGS 84) for all geography columns
    14в†’
    15в†’---
    16в†’
    17в†’## Schemas
    18в†’
    19в†’| Schema         | Purpose                                                              |
    20в†’|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
    21в†’| `search_base`  | Core institution data, datasets, rankings, materialized views        |
    22в†’| `location`     | US states, institution aliases, ZIP code coordinates (PostGIS)       |
    23в†’| `subscription` | Teams, team members, subscriptions, purchases                        |
    24в†’| `ugc`          | User-generated content: saved institutions, saved searches, prefs    |
    25в†’| `internal`     | Admin event log and user feedback                                    |

   405в†’## Materialized Views
   406в†’
   407в†’### `search_base.dataset_merged`
   408в†’
   409в†’**Primary search target.** Denormalized join of `institution` + up to 5 years
   410в†’of `annual_dataset` + all `dataset` rows, grouped per institution.
   411в†’
   412в†’**Construction logic** (migration v2):
   413в†’
   414в†’1. Select `annual_dataset` rows where `year > current_year - 5`, ordered by year.
   415в†’2. Aggregate per `(unit_id, theme, datakey)`: merge up to 5 yearly JSONB objects
   416в†’   via concatenation (later years override earlier keys).
   417в†’3. UNION with non-annual `dataset` rows.
   418в†’4. JOIN with `institution` and `location.state` for full name/location data.
   419в†’5. Aggregate all datakeys into a single `data` JSONB object per institution via
   420в†’   `jsonb_object_agg(datakey, data)`.
   421в†’
   422в†’**Computed columns**:
   423в†’
   424в†’| Column                          | Type       | Source                                         |
   425в†’|---------------------------------|------------|------------------------------------------------|
   426в†’| `unit_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.unit_id`                          |
   427в†’| `ope6_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.ope6_id`                          |
   428в†’| `ope8_id`                       | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.ope8_id`                          |
   429в†’| `institution_name`              | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.institution_name`                 |
   430в†’| `city`                          | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.city`                             |
   431в†’| `state`                         | `VARCHAR`  | `institution.state`                            |
   432в†’| `coordinates`                   | `geography`| `institution.coordinates`                      |
   433в†’| `longitude`                     | `float`    | `ST_X(coordinates::geometry)`                  |
   434в†’| `latitude`                      | `float`    | `ST_Y(coordinates::geometry)`                  |
   435в†’| `name_search_vector`            | `tsvector` | `to_tsvector('english', institution_name)`     |
   436в†’| `name_location_search_vector`   | `tsvector` | `to_tsvector('english', name + city + state + state_full_name)` |
   437в†’| `data`                          | `JSONB`    | `jsonb_object_agg(datakey, data)` from combined datasets |
   438в†’
   439в†’**Created with**: `WITH NO DATA` -- must be explicitly refreshed after data loads.
   440в†’

   525в†’## Foreign Key Map
   526в†’
   527в†’All foreign keys use `ON DELETE RESTRICT ON UPDATE CASCADE` unless noted otherwise.
   528в†’
   529в†’```
   530в†’location.state
   531в†’  <- search_base.institution(state)
   532в†’
   533в†’search_base.institution
   534в†’  <- search_base.dataset(unit_id)
   535в†’  <- search_base.annual_dataset(unit_id)
   536в†’  <- search_base.institution_alias(unit_id)
   537в†’  <- location.institution_normalized_alias(unit_id)
   538в†’  <- ugc.saved_institution(unit_id)
   539в†’
   540в†’subscription.team
   541в†’  <- subscription.team_member(team_id)
   542в†’  <- subscription.subscription_slot(for_team_id)
   543в†’  <- ugc.saved_institution(team_id)
   544в†’  <- ugc.saved_search(team_id)
   545в†’  <- ugc.preference(team_id)          [ON DELETE CASCADE]
   546в†’
   547в†’subscription.subscription
   548в†’  <- subscription.subscription_slot(subscription_id)
   549в†’```

   566в†’
   567в†’## Data Flow
   568в†’
   569в†’1. **Ingestion**: Raw data from IPEDS and other sources is loaded into
   570в†’   `institution` (JSONB columns), `dataset` (theme-scoped), and
   571в†’   `annual_dataset` (year-scoped) tables.
   572в†’
   573в†’2. **Materialization**: The `dataset_merged` materialized view denormalizes
   574в†’   institution + dataset + annual_dataset into a single searchable row per
   575в†’   institution. The view merges up to 5 years of annual data (later years
   576в†’   override earlier keys). Must be refreshed after any data load via
   577в†’   `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW search_base.dataset_merged`.
   578в†’
   579в†’3. **Search**: The search engine (`api/internal/search`) queries
   580в†’   `dataset_merged` exclusively. Filters translate to parameterized SQL
   581в†’   against the `data` JSONB column, tsvector columns, geography column,
   582в†’   and top-level columns. GIN indexes on `data` and tsvector columns
   583в†’   provide fast filtering; SP-GiST on `coordinates` enables spatial queries.
   584в†’
   585в†’4. **User data**: Authenticated users are assigned a personal team
   586в†’   (via `EnsureUser` middleware). Teams own `saved_institution`,
   587в†’   `saved_search`, and `preference` records in the `ugc` schema.
   588в†’
   589в†’5. **Subscriptions**: The `subscription` schema tracks commercial
   590в†’   subscriptions, slots allocated to teams, and purchase transactions.
